key_risks: AI/semiconductor rebound could drive cap-weighted indexes higher, leaving equal-weight tilt behind
Hot June CPI on July 14 could lift yields and hurt long Treasuries and rate-sensitive sectors
Iran/Hormuz escalation could spike oil and pressure equity risk appetite broadly
Biotech and regional banks at 52w highs face reversal risk on any credit or clinical disappointment
July FOMC could surprise hawkish, pressuring duration and small/regional exposures

portfolio.rationale: EMA approvals catalyst, defensive with strong recent breadth, low beta supports outperformance amid AI/mega-cap weakness.
Steeper curve, declining claims, strong Q1 GDP revision, and breakout near 52w highs support continuation through July earnings.
Strong M&A/approval backdrop with EMA catalyst, breaking out at 52w highs with improving sentiment.
Broad market participation (2/3 stocks up) favors equal-weight over cap-weighted while AI mega-caps wobble.
Yields easing, soft sentiment data, contained inflation expectations, and FOMC minutes catalyst support duration.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward defensive/breadth winners (healthcare, biotech, equal-weight, regional banks benefiting from steeper curve) while AI mega-caps face Micron-led weakness. Long duration hedges growth risk with FOMC minutes and CPI catalysts in window.
rationale_summary: Rotate from concentrated AI leadership into broadening sectors with catalysts (healthcare EMA approvals, biotech breakout, regional banks) plus duration hedge as yields ease.
